history.text: Guatemala declared independence from the Spanish Empire.
Declared from the First Mexican Empire ruled or strongly influenced the territory before the modern state.
Declared from the Federal Republic of Central America introduced a new form of state rule.
The current constitution came into force, setting the basic framework for today's Guatemala.

worthSeeing.title: Lake Atitlan:
worthSeeing.text: Three volcanoes rising from a highland lake - described as the most beautiful in the world.

worthSeeing.title: Antigua:
worthSeeing.text: A colonial UNESCO city of pastel churches, cobblestone streets and volcano backdrops.

worthSeeing.title: Tikal:
worthSeeing.text: A Maya city-state rising from the jungle, with temples above the rainforest canopy.
